name: Ubiquiti airMAX PowerBeam 5AC 620
title: Ubiquiti airMAX PowerBeam 5AC 620
short_description: PowerBeam AC, 5GHz, 620mm, 29dBi, 2x2 MIMO antenna, AirOS, POE-RJ-45, 802.11ac
description: The airMAX PowerBeam 5AC 620, distinguished by its model manufacture part number PBE-5AC-620, is a premier long-range wireless network bridge developed by Ubiquiti Networks, a leader in high-performance networking technology. This device is part of the airMAX series, designed to deliver superior point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) connectivity for high-speed, broadband wireless networks. Operating within the 5 GHz frequency band, the PowerBeam 5AC 620 utilizes Ubiquiti's proprietary airMAX ac technology to maximize throughput, reduce latency, and provide scalable network solutions.

detailed_description.heading: 5 GHz Long-Distance WiFi Antenna
detailed_description.text: The airMAX PowerBeam 5AC 620, distinguished by its model manufacture part number PBE-5AC-620, is a premier long-range wireless network bridge developed by Ubiquiti Networks, a leader in high-performance networking technology. This device is part of the airMAX series, designed to deliver superior point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) connectivity for high-speed, broadband wireless networks. Operating within the 5 GHz frequency band, the PowerBeam 5AC 620 utilizes Ubiquiti's proprietary airMAX ac technology to maximize throughput, reduce latency, and provide scalable network solutions.

detailed_description.heading: Important Note
detailed_description.text: This product should be purchased in pairs in order to form links.

detailed_description.heading: Compatibility
detailed_description.text: The PowerBeam 5AC 620 is specifically designed to work in conjunction with other airMAX ac devices. This includes various models of Ubiquiti’s outdoor wireless access points, bridges, and customer premises equipment (CPE) that also utilize airMAX ac technology. It's also important to consider, that while the PowerBeam 5AC 620 is optimized for the airMAX series, direct operational integration with the UniFi series, which is tailored for managed Wi-Fi networking within indoor and semi-outdoor environments and uses a different management system (UniFi Controller), is not its primary feature. The distinct operational frameworks and intended applications of the airMAX and UniFi series limit their direct interoperability.

detailed_description.heading: Design
detailed_description.text: Central to the PowerBeam 5AC 620's design is its impressive 620 mm diameter dish reflector, which significantly enhances the antenna's gain to 29 dBi and focus. This larger reflector size allows for concentrated signal strength over extended distances, minimizing signal degradation and interference, which is critical for maintaining robust and reliable wireless connections across vast expanses. The device's ability to direct a focused beam of RF energy towards its target not only improves the quality and speed of the data transmission but also reduces the potential for interference from other sources in densely populated RF environments.

detailed_description.heading: Technology
detailed_description.text: Incorporating advanced airMAX ac technology, the PowerBeam 5AC 620 benefits from a sophisticated modulation scheme that supports higher throughput rates than traditional Wi-Fi standards. This is achieved through the use of more efficient data encoding, which increases the amount of data transmitted in each radio wave. Furthermore, the airMAX ac technology introduces an improved Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) protocol, which organizes data transmissions more effectively, reducing collisions and ensuring more efficient use of the available bandwidth.

detailed_description.heading: Deployment
detailed_description.text: The PowerBeam 5AC 620 is engineered for ease of deployment and flexibility. It features a unique mounting bracket designed for easy installation and precise alignment, allowing network operators to quickly set up the device and optimize its position for the best signal quality. This focus on deployment efficiency makes the PowerBeam 5AC 620 a favored choice for network installations requiring rapid rollout without compromising on performance or reliability. Additionally, Ubiquiti’s InnerFeed® technology integrates the radio into the feedhorn of an antenna, so there is no need for cables to connect the radio to the antenna. This improves performance because it eliminates cable losses. The PowerBeam 5AC requires no tools for assembly; only a single wrench is required for pole-mounting.

detailed_description.heading: Durability
detailed_description.text: Durability is a hallmark of Ubiquiti's design philosophy, and the PowerBeam 5AC 620 is no exception. It is housed in a rugged, weather-resistant enclosure capable of withstanding adverse outdoor conditions. This ensures the device's longevity and continuous operation in environments ranging from scorching heat of max 70 °C to freezing max -40 °C temperatures, making it an ideal solution for outdoor wireless networking across diverse geographic locations.

detailed_description.heading: Security and Management
detailed_description.text:
Security is also a critical aspect of the PowerBeam 5AC 620's design. The device supports WPA2 encryption standards, ensuring that data transmitted across the network is protected against unauthorized access and eavesdropping. This level of security is essential for maintaining the integrity and confidentiality of sensitive information flowing through the network.

Management of the PowerBeam 5AC 620 is facilitated through Ubiquiti's airOS, an intuitive, web-based operating system that provides comprehensive tools for device configuration, network monitoring, and system diagnostics. airOS enables network administrators to access detailed analytics, fine-tune network parameters, and manage devices remotely, ensuring optimal network performance and reliability.

detailed_description.heading: Selecting the Right Product
detailed_description.text:
Selecting the right PowerBeam product from Ubiquiti for your network involves a nuanced understanding of your specific needs and environmental conditions. Consider the frequency band: 2.4 GHz models like the PBE-2AC-400 and PBE-M2-400 are adept at penetrating obstacles and are suitable for medium-range deployments, while 5 GHz models, including PBE-5AC-Gen2, PBE-5AC-500, PBE-5AC-620, and PBE-5AC-ISO-Gen2, offer higher throughput and are ideal for clearer, longer-range links. The choice between these depends on the balance between range, throughput, and environmental factors such as obstacles and interference.

Antenna size directly influences range and signal focus: larger dishes on the PBE-5AC-500 and PBE-5AC-620 support the longest distances, whereas the PBE-5AC-Gen2 provides versatile deployment options. The PBE-5AC-ISO-Gen2's enhanced noise isolation makes it perfect for high-interference environments. Lastly, deployment scenarios—whether seeking broad coverage for multiple locations or a focused beam for direct point-to-point links—will guide your selection. Budget considerations and future scalability also play crucial roles in determining the most cost-effective and performance-optimized choice for your wireless network infrastructure.
